steps.title: Identify what the phrase suggests
steps.content: The phrase “every conceivable obstacle” suggests the author means all obstacles that can be imagined or thought of. The word “every” emphasizes completeness across all possibilities, making the meaning of “conceivable” the key signal.

steps.title: Match the meaning to choice A
steps.content: The target meaning is something that is possible or can be imagined. “Conceivable” comes from “conceive,” meaning to think of or imagine, so it directly expresses the idea of something that is possible to imagine. Choice A (“possible”) captures this. B (regular), C (visible), and D (basic) do not express this sense of imaginability. That matches choice A — A is correct.
